title: Default podman-machine-default on Windows missing default `seccomp.json`?
body:
### Issue Description

I use `podman` from my WSL (Arch) using the shared socket mount + `podman-remote`. I was experimenting today with some `molecule` stuff, and the build was failing with an error related to a missing `/etc/containers/seccomp.json`. 

I copied the default `seccomp.json` from my arch WSL to `/etc/containers` of the podman-machine-default and things seem to work.

Is it expected that the `seccomp.json` is missing?

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Run a custom build with molecule which builds a custom `archlinux:latest` image `molecule create`


### Describe the results you received

opening seccomp profile failed: open /etc/containers/seccomp.json: no such file or directory

### Describe the results you expected

`/etc/containers/seccomp.json` is available in the WSL VM used by podman?

### podman info output

### Privileged Or Rootless

Yes

### Additional environment details

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body: This sounds like a bug podman-remote should not read `/etc/containers/seccomp.json` on the client

Yes I agree the default seccomp.json file should only be read inside of the Machine.   One issue might be how well we handle

podman run --security-opt seccomp:/tmp/myseccomp.json ...

In this case the myseccomp.json should be read from the client and sent to the host.
The current API does not allow for sending a seccomp as such the path can only ever be read on the server. It is no different then --volume, --mount.

> This sounds like a bug podman-remote should not read /etc/containers/seccomp.json on the client

Not sure I'm understanding, but I think that's what's happening? The problem is that in `podman-remote-default:/etc/containers` there's no `seccomp.json`

Hello, I just tried to build on another Windows PC and it reproduces the problem.

```
❯ podman-remote build  /home/rdb/.cache/molecule/extensions/default/ -f /home/rdb/.cache/molecule/extensions/default/Dockerfile_archlinux_latest
STEP 1/2: FROM archlinux:latest
STEP 2/2: RUN pacman-key --init && pacman -Syu --noconfirm && pacman -S --noconfirm python readline
Error: building at STEP "RUN pacman-key --init && pacman -Syu --noconfirm && pacman -S --noconfirm python readline": opening seccomp profile failed: open /etc/containers/seccomp.json: no such file or directory
```

Is it a bug? I think it is...

just to be clear are you talking about the client side or server side?

/etc/containers/seccomp.json will not exits by default that is for admins to overwrite the default profile that we use, our rpm packaging should ship and read the default from /usr/share/containers/seccomp.json if there is no file under /etc.

The remote client should not read this file as only the server needs it so as long as it exits on the server it should work

Thanks @Luap99. Then there's something not working here. 

When  I copied the `/etc/containers.json` from my client WSL (Archlinux) to the `podman-machine-default` WSL VM to `/etc/containers/seccomp.json`, the error stops occurring. 

So it seems `seccomp.json` is not being read from `/usr/share/containers/seccomp.json` on the server side.

Please update your podman version on the server, i.e. recreate the podman machine or run dnf update in the machine.

I read the code on main and do not see that we ever try to read the path on the client so I think that works fine. Is this specific to podman build? Because I only tested podman run.

yeah ok I think the issue is in the buildah code. The client side there checks /etc/containers/seccomp.json and /usr/share/containers/seccomp.json and then sends this path to the server. The client should not send such defaults to the server as the server must use its own defaults

On the machine: 

```
[user@Baaj ~]$ podman --version
podman version 5.2.3
```

and indeed it's specific to `podman build`.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Just a note that this is still a problem. I configured the same thing brand new on a pc and that doesn't work with the same error.

The problem is still present in 5.6.0. I fixed it by copying the default over myself.

    cp /usr/share/containers/seccomp.json /etc/containers/seccomp.json
